-- 1 of 9 --

Launch Story Themes
!
Now I want to drill down into some of the common story themes that we use in our
product launches.
!
These themes have been proven over the years and they work really well. They keep
people engaged, they pull people into your market and they will draw them into your
launch and into your world. One great big thing about these themes is they really help
humanize our marketing.
!
When you use these themes, people will really start to identify with you. In fact, they
will even want to be you. One of the things that these stories do is they really put us in
the same plain as the people watching our launch, intellectually, emotionally, and
socially. The deal is that a lot of times if you’re out there, just having a business sets
you apart.
!
It sort of turns you into a guru or a star or some type of an unattainable person. You
don’t really want that. You want people to be able to connect with you. You want that
expert guru positioning. You want to be seen as special but you also want them to be
able to connect with you. These stories will really allow you to do that. You’ll see as we
go through these.
!
Think about the movie Star Wars for example. That movie is a classic hero’s journey –
Joseph Campbell’s hero’s journey. In fact, George Lucas, who created the movies, based
Star Wars on that hero’s journey. In any case, Luke Skywalker ends up being an
incredible hero in that movie, but he’s the “every man.” He starts off as just a boy and
then he grows into this hero so you can identify with him.
!
One thing that is important to remember as you go through these themes is that you
can mix and match these themes.
!
You can use different themes in different parts of your launch. You can use more than
one theme. You can have parts of your launch that don’t have a theme at all. However,
I’ll just say this, the more you put these themes to work both in your launch and in your
entire business, the better. They are extremely powerful. The more you base your story
or your launch off of them, the better you’re going to do.
!
One final thing, I am a huge fan of being truthful in my marketing. It just makes
everything a lot easier and more congruent. Authenticity really does win. People have
their “B.S. detector” and it’s a finely tuned mechanism. They can see right through
inauthenticity like you wouldn’t believe. I’m a big fan of using these stories truthfully.
!

PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 2 of 9 --

The good news is that these themes exist everywhere in our lives and you’re not going
to have to look very hard to find a way to put these themes to work in your business.
It’s often just a matter of looking at our lives in the context of these themes and then
finding the ones that apply or don’t apply at any given time.
!
Let’s get started. I’m going to start with my absolute favorite. This is my go-to theme.
One that I probably use more than any of the others. It is the reluctant hero.
!
I’ve used this one in some of my biggest, most successful launches. It works really well.
I think one of the reasons I love this one is that it is so aligned with my personality and
my life. This Reluctant Hero story theme was the basis of my first two really big six
figure launches back when I was publishing about the stock market.
!
One of them was when I published a home study course for stock market traders. The
deal is that I had been publishing a stock market advisory letter for years. That was my
business – publishing a newsletter. Basically we were putting out this letter saying this
is what’s going to happen in the market. Our staff was really accurate. We published an
amazing newsletter. However, people wanted to know how to do it.
!
I started writing a book and that book literally took me years to write. It was at least
three years. It was just ridiculous how long it took me to finish that thing. I would never
do it that way again. I learned a lot in the process but it took me forever to complete.
The writing and research was hard. I really didn’t want to do it. I just wanted to publish
a newsletter. I’m a lifestyle-focused person. I love having a business. I love what the
business does for me and my lifestyle. I’m very much about lifestyle.
!
When it was finally almost done and it was time to do the launch, The reluctant hero
story was just a natural. Basically, I’m just going to tell you this story as if you were
one of my prospects.
What I essentially said was...I just got this email the other day and it was from one of
our long time readers. He’s been following our market letter for a couple of years now.
!
He said , ‘Jeff, I love your updates. You’re uncanny how accurate you are. Your support
and resistance levels are amazing. The market is always turning right where you say it’s
going to turn. Can you teach me to do that?’ This is not the first time I’ve gotten that
request. In fact, almost since the time when we started publishing, we’ve had people
that keep writing in. They say, ‘Jeff, I love your stuff. I keep following it but I want to
know how you do what you do. Who knows? Maybe one day you’re going to decide to
retire. I depend on these numbers and I really want to know how you do it. How can
you be so accurate?’
!
Like I said, we’ve been getting these requests forever. In fact, people ask me, ‘Can you
just put together a training program? Can you write a book on this? Can you put
together a video course that shows me how to do it?’
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module2v1.0

-- 3 of 9 --

In fact, I literally have a folder in my email where I just put all those hundreds of
requests of people asking for this. I would love to do it. It’s something I guess I’ve
always thought about doing. The thing is, we’re really busy just putting out this
newsletter and I’m a lifestyle-focused person. As you know, I live in Colorado. I love
spending time with my family. I love spending time outdoors. How do I carve out the
time to do this?
!
Finally, I got that 100 th email, that heartfelt email from one of our clients that has been
with us forever. That made me say, ‘I’m just going to sit down and I’m going to knock
this out. For a couple of weeks, I’m going to write out the training manual and we’ll put
it out there and be all set.’ I actually started to do it. Unfortunately, it became a much
bigger project than I thought. In fact, two weeks ended up being two months. Then it
extended even further. Frankly, if I wasn’t so far into it I would have just given up. I
was so close to being done – at least I thought I was.
!
What I did was I just cranked through the thing and now we’re at the point where I’m
actually almost done with this. Looking back, I just can’t believe how much effort it’s
been and how long it’s taken me to do. I almost got this project done. Of course, after I
wrote the book, that wasn’t enough and I decided we needed some videos to go along
with it. I recorded some videos. Then I needed to round it out with the mindset. I did
some interviews with top traders on mindset. Now we’re almost done.
!
In fact, I’m almost about to send it off to my editor. Just one question for you. That is
after all this work, after hundreds of hours into this thing. I just need to know that we
covered everything and that we’ve nailed it completely down. This is where it comes to
you. I would really love if you could just hit your reply button now and tell me what the
two most important things are that I need to cover in this training course.
!
If you could do that for me right now, I would really appreciate it. Like I said, I never
would have done this thing in the first place if I had realized how much effort I had to
put into it. Now that we’re near the finish line, I just want to make it perfect.”
!
That’s a little bit of the reluctant hero. I slipped in a little bit of a prelaunch type of thing
right at the end where I actually ask for feedback.
!
That’s just an example and you could use those throughout your launch. The reluctant
hero theme works really well any time there’s some level of market demand for a
product before you actually create it. I used the reluctant hero again before I rolled out
my first version of Product Launch Formula – the version 1.0 back in 2005.
!
At that point, I had taught the basics of Product Launch Formula to a number of the top
online business gurus and internet marketing gurus of the day, specifically, John Reese,
Frank Kern, and Yanik Silver. I had helped them out before I ever published Product
Launch Formula or anything like that.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module1v1.0

-- 4 of 9 --

The word actually started to get out that I helped these guys behind the scenes. Then
John Reese did the first million dollar launch. He did it in a single day. Then the next
day, he put out a report that publicly thanked me for teaching him my launch
techniques.
!
The day he put out that report, people started calling me and asking for help. I didn’t
have a marketing presence. I wasn’t teaching about marketing. I was still teaching
about the stock market. I didn’t even have a phone number on my website. Somehow
people were tracking down my phone number and all of a sudden the phone’s ringing
off the hook.
!
At that point, my life was very simple. I was just publishing my stock market letter. I
didn’t even have to talk to people. I didn’t have a customer service staff. I didn’t have
any staff whatsoever; it was just me – just one person in this really simple business. I
wasn’t ready to start talking to people, or start consulting or coaching people. That was
the truth of it. It also set up a very nice reluctant hero kind of story. A year later when I
ended up creating Product Launch Formula, and started teaching about Product Launch
Formula, it was just natural that I tell this reluctant hero story.
!
I helped out some of the big gurus, and the word snuck out. People started writing in
and asking me. My phone was ringing off the hook. Finally, the only way I could stop
that phone from ringing off the hook was to actually put this course together and start
teaching it. There’s that reluctant hero. I didn’t want to do this, but I was dragged
kicking and screaming. When you do this, the “hero” part isn’t like you’re beating your
chest like some great big hero. The “hero” part is that you have the answer, the
solution.
!
You have something that could transform your viewer’s lives. It’s like you’re the new
sheriff in town and you’re coming in to get rid of all the bad guys. Actually, you’re the
new sheriff and you’ve got this great solution that’s going to help their lives. You don’t
really want to have to go through all the effort of putting this solution out and
supporting it. However, people really want this from you. They really need this help.
They need that transformation in their lives, that change, and that solution.
!
You putting in the effort to create that solution is the part that makes you the hero. The
reluctance is that you didn’t want to have to do the work, and didn’t want to have to
put yourself in the limelight. You’re doing it almost as a service to people. That’s the
essence of The Reluctant Hero story. It’s very powerful. It’s basically my go-to story
because it really fixed my life.
!
The reality is that I really am constantly torn between building my business and my
impact further, spending more time with my family, and more time in the mountains
near my home.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 5

-- 5 of 9 --

The reluctant hero – I’m a big fan of that one. It’s worked really well for me. I’ve seen it
work for a lot of other people when they apply a product launch formula to use in their
business.
!
The next theme is “loss and redemption.” The reason I’ve got these stars up here is
these are my “double-starred” favorites. Loss and redemption – this is a really powerful
story. Maybe you had something great and for some reason you lost it. It’s even better
if someone took it away from you, and you turned around and came back with it bigger,
better, and more powerful.
!
There are a lot of Hollywood movies with this theme. A lot of times you see this in
business, especially when someone is teaching about business. Perhaps they had a
business then lost the business. They went bankrupt, and came back and started again.
Or, maybe you were an athlete, got injured and came back after your injury. Maybe you
were a guitar player, hurt your hand and had to recover. During that recovery, you
learned things about teaching guitar and you were able to teach other people.
!
Loss and redemption turns you into a very sympathetic character. People are going to
root for you because you once had something and you lost it, especially if you lost it in
some unjust or unfair fashion. They’re going to root for you to get that back. Loss and
redemption is very powerful. It’s often a little harder to tie into a product launch than
the reluctant hero.
!
Often it’s a smaller part of the launch. In other words, it’s a part of the bigger story that
we’ll work into loss and redemption. If you chance using this in your launch, it is an
absolutely great theme.
Closely related to loss and redemption is the underdog story. People love to root for
an underdog.
!
That’s really what’s behind the loss and redemption. This is usually not something that
you’re going to base an entire launch around. Often you can turn yourself into the
underdog. For example, let’s say you’re fighting something in the industry – like maybe
an industry association that’s trying to hold people down, the IRS or some type of a
common enemy.
!
The common enemy is an amazing thing. People go to war and they kill each other over
a common enemy. This actually ties into revenge and that’s something we’re going to
get to a little further. You can’t do any better in your marketing than to have someone
rooting for you. If you could turn yourself into the underdog, it’s really powerful.
!
The next one is discovery. This is one we use along with curation. These next two fit
really well with product launches. Discovery is basically your origin story. I shared
earlier with you in the last video a little bit of my origin story. I shared how I learned
about information marketing. The origin story is really powerful. People want to know
how you got to where you are. How did you come to create whatever product you
created? How did you come to launch that product?
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 6
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 6 of 9 --

!
I just told you a little bit about my origin story at PLF and how I came to do that. I told
it within the context of the reluctant hero. If I was telling that further and was really
going into discovery, I would talk about how I learned to do these launches. I have a
whole story about that. How did I learn? I didn’t learn them from anyone. I had to
make it up.
!
The reason I made up these launches is because I didn’t know anything about sales,
selling or marketing. I just invented a new way to do it. I was scared to ask for the
order because I was scared to sell. When I first started out, instead of asking people to
give me money and asking for the sale, I kept giving them more cool stuff so they
would love me.
!
I finally worked up the nerve to sell to them. At it turns out, that ended up being my
product launch formula. That ended up being prelaunch content delivering great stuff
ahead of time. That’s my 10second version of my discovery story. Discovery is an origin
story and it works really well.
!
There’s also the curation model. The curation theme is another origin story. It’s the
mad scientist coming up with some discovery. The curation is where you studied all the
great teachers, thought leaders, and gurus. Maybe you read all of the books and the
training in your market or niche. A perfect example of this is Napoleon Hill with his
book, Think and Grow Rich, and everything that followed after that.
!
In this case, he interviewed the 50 most successful men in the world. There’s a whole
story about how he got to do that. He interviewed all of these really successful people.
Then you see these two themes play out over and over, both the discovery and
curation. They are both versions of an origin story.
!
Usually, your entire launch will be based on either the discovery or the curation. Often
that starts off your prelaunch – it’s the beginning. I’m sure you see how you can mix
and match these. I showed you how my origin story and reluctant hero can be basically
tied together. You use the discovery story within the reluctant hero story.
!
Next is revenge. This really isn’t something that I use very much. I don’t really like it
because I think that if you’re seeking revenge it does not make you terribly attractive.
!
The bottom line in our type of business, and any type of business, is that you want to
be naturally attractive. We want to attract people to us. We want to attract prospects
and clients. If you look at someone who’s going after revenge, it’s not a very attractive
way to be.
!
It’s easy to be very unattractive when you’re using revenge as a plot line. Revenge is an
extremely powerful emotion. It does make for a powerful story, but you have to be
careful. It’s usually used in marketing where there is a common enemy. The common
enemy can be an association, organization or a government agency. They’re big, nasty
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 7
PLF_Module2v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 7 of 9 --

competitors who really try to shut you out or keep you down, and keep you out of the
market.
!
What you want to do if you’re going to use one of these revenge plot lines, is you need
to make sure that your revenge aligns with your prospects and their self-interest. If
your prospect wants something really badly, you have to make sure that the revenge
you’re going after also gets them the thing they want. At the end of your revenge story,
you really want it to grow beyond revenge. You want to say you see a bigger picture
and move towards service.
!
You want to go away from just hurting someone or getting back at someone. Maybe
that’s your initial impetuous but as you go forward, now you’ve grown and you really
just want to serve people. Otherwise, revenge is a real problem. There’s this big, bad
organization. Maybe it’s an industry or an organization doing some nasty things to keep
people back.
!
If your revenge story can help your prospects get rid of all those bad things, then you
align yourself with your prospects. Although you want to make sure that it’s not just
about the revenge but more about the good you’re going to do for that market. Frankly,
that’s why I like the underdog better. The underdog and the revenge can be very
similar. Those themes are really closely related. I would tend to go more for the
underdog one than the revenge one.
The final one here is ascension. This is a really powerful theme. This is basically the
rags to riches story. We’re fascinated by people who rise from humble beginnings and
then rise to prominence. We never get sick of that story and it just really fascinates you.
If you’re going to use ascension it tends to be a very personal story. It tends to be
about you.
!
Some people are really less comfortable sharing their personal story. If that’s you then
you’re going to have to watch out with the Ascension story. If you use the ascension
story, you’re really sharing a story about yourself. My story is very much an ascension
story. I’ve used it over and over. I’ve used it in combination with the reluctant hero. It is
rags to riches for me. I started out with nothing and I’ve built this amazing business.
!
I didn’t use the ascension story when I was publishing about the stock market. I really
didn’t have an ascension story. When I started publishing Product Launch Formula,
what I was publishing about was building an online business. I started from nothing
and built a great online business.
!
Now if you’re going to use ascension story, it’s best if there’s some type of motivation.
There needs to be something that drives you to want to make that change. There
should be something that drives you to really make that ascension and pick yourself up
and say, “I gotta make a change. I gotta start down that road that takes me from rags
to riches.”
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 8
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 8 of 9 --

The initial catalyst for me was as I told you earlier. I was at home taking care of a
couple of kids. We had $400 per year disposable income. I was a stay-at-home dad and
my wife really wanted to come home. She was miserable. I had to find a way to change
my family’s lives. That’s how I started out. That was a big catalyst for me. It wasn’t just
me wanting to get rich. I had this whole idea of helping my family out. That really helps
out this ascension story.
!
Often your character or hero will have some major change in motivation over time in
this ascension story. For me, initially it was to support my family. Then it was eventually
to help entrepreneurs and even change the world through helping entrepreneurs. There
you have it. They are the seven main themes we’ve used over again in Product Launch
Formula.
!
Again, you don’t usually follow one all the way through and hammer it through for
every single video or piece of prelaunch. For all those pieces, you can drop them here
or there and often you will have one overarching theme like that reluctant hero or this
ascension Story. Even the discovery or the curation is very powerful stuff. The more you
can work these kinds of themes into your launch, the better you’re going to be.
